2606.11836.title: Towards Data-free and Training-free Compression for Speech Foundation Models Using Parameter Clustering
2606.11836.paper_summary: This paper presents a novel data-free and training-free compression approach for speech foundation models using channelwise clustering via k-means. More fine-grained, mixed sparsity pruning by layer-level varying number of parameter clusters is also explored. Experiments conducted on the LibriSpeech dataset suggest that when operating with pruning sparsity of 50% on HuBERT-large, consistent WER reductions of 27.73%/18.61% absolute (34.37%/21.91% relative) over the magnitude-based pruning were obtained on the test-clean and test-other subsets before fine-tuning and 0.19%/0.79% absolute (3.36%/4.62% relative) after fine-tuning with only 3 epochs. Similar WER reductions of 2.86%/5.02% absolute (59.21%/55.29% relative) were observed against magnitudebased pruning on Whisper-large-v3 at 10% sparsity, all with no significant WER increase relative to the uncompressed baseline.
2606.11836.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文提出了一種新穎的無數據和無訓練的語音基礎模型壓縮方法，通過 k-means 進行通道聚類。還探討了基於層級的參數聚類數量變化的更細緻的混合稀疏修剪。對 LibriSpeech 數據集進行的實驗表明，在 HuBERT-large 上以 50% 的修剪稀疏運行時，測試清晰集和測試其他集在微調之前，與基於幅度的修剪相比，獲得了 27.73%/18.61% 的絕對 WER 減少（34.37%/21.91% 相對），而在僅進行 3 次訓練週期的微調後，絕對 WER 減少為 0.19%/0.79%（相對 3.36%/4.62%）。在 Whisper-large-v3 上以 10% 的稀疏性，觀察到相對於基於幅度的修剪，絕對 WER 減少為 2.86%/5.02%（相對 59.21%/55.29%），而且相對於未壓縮基線沒有顯著的 WER 增加。
2606.11836.author: Haoning Xu et.al.
2606.11836.authors: Haoning Xu, Zhaoqing Li, Huimeng Wang, Youjun Chen, Chengxi Deng, Mengzhe Geng, Xunying Liu
